text: The surgeon carefully dissects the thymus gland located in the chest cavity, meticulously separating it from surrounding tissues.

Very interesting, this girl actually presented with difficulty in walking down the stairs. So why would I dig into her chest if she couldn't walk down the stairs? Well friends, she couldn't walk down the stairs because of a condition known as myasthenia gravis.

Myasthenia gravis means a very severe and grave weakness of the muscles. Why were her muscles weak? Because inside this thymus there was a tumor that was releasing some special antibodies. What were those antibodies doing? They were attacking her junctions of nerves and muscles, the so-called neuromuscular junction, and making her very, very weak.
Therefore, the thymoma or tumor in the thymus had to be removed along with the rest of the thymus.

The surgeon performs a thymectomy using a minimally invasive approach with three small incisions in the chest. Ultrasonic shears are employed to gently dissect and remove the thymus and tumor, utilizing ultrasound energy to cut tissue without causing thermal damage.
The heart is visibly pumping beneath the tumor as the surgeon continues the dissection of the thymus gland, maintaining awareness of the heart's proximity during the procedure.

But on the contrary, this poor girl kept slipping back onto the ventilator, which means she was not able to breathe for herself. So what did we do? A little bit of research showed us that she could be having long-acting antibodies released much earlier by the thymus.

Knowing this, we decided to exchange and take out all plasma in her blood because the plasma was probably carrying these antibodies when we completely took out the plasma and did what is called a plasma pharesis within twenty four hours this young girl was back to normal and we could finally take out the tube once and for all

namely the tube that was allowing her to breathe through the ventilator we could take that off free her from the ventilator dependence that she was earlier going to and sort her problem out completely so this is the thoracoscopic thymectomy or removal of the thymus gland in total

being done for a young girl with a tumor in the chest. Thymomas present in many ways, but the commonest way in which they present is with a tumor in the chest and also with a difficulty in walking, difficulty in breathing, especially as
The surgeon gently dissects the entire thymus glandular structure, carefully navigating around a large vein visible in the surgical field to prevent bleeding from smaller veins.
And this is one of the few cases reported in medical literature. We have subsequently published this in the Indian Journal of Endocrine Surgery.

We are happy to say that one of the first in the world was performed by ourselves at Lifeline Institute of Minimal Access Keyhole Surgery at on on was done um with the patient on general anesthetic and the patient had a wonderful recovery and is now completely well one and a half years after surgery surgery done thoracoscopic thymectomy at lifeline institute of minimal access in kilpock chennai
